goal: Fix the Excel export redirect behavior and clarify where operations-center capabilities live in the product.
deliverable: A frontend UX correction set that replaces popup-based downloads with blob downloads and moves operations-center functionality into its own main navigation view.
constraints: Keep the existing FastAPI + static Vue architecture
Preserve current backend export endpoints unless a frontend-only fix is sufficient
Do not redesign unrelated pages or create new operations-center data domains
acceptance_criteria: Excel export no longer relies on window.open or browser redirection to 127.0.0.1
Export requests still include the current ledger filters
Supplier, price, inventory, import-task, invoice, and notification features are no longer embedded inside system settings
Settings copy clearly communicates that business operations have moved to a dedicated operations area
product_acceptance_criteria: The main navigation exposes a dedicated operations-center destination
The operations page copy explains what the page is for instead of using internal acceptance language
System settings stays focused on AI, backup, WebDAV, and maintenance concerns
manual_spot_checks: Trigger Excel export from a filtered ledger view and confirm the download starts without opening a localhost tab
Open the new operations-center navigation item and confirm the supplier, price, inventory, import, invoice, and notification sections appear there
Open system settings and confirm the embedded operations panel is gone
completion_language_policy: Full completion wording is allowed only after project validation passes and the frontend evidence shows blob-download behavior plus the new operations-page structure.
delivery_truth_contract: Do not claim a full desktop browser click-through; claim only the validation that was actually run: project validation, a temporary QuickJS helper smoke for the shared download flow, and template-level structure checks.
non_goals: A full visual redesign of the application shell
inferred_assumptions: The reported 127.0.0.1 problem comes from popup-based file download behavior inside desktop usage
A first-level navigation entry is a reasonable placement for the operations-center feature set
Backup downloads should follow the same controlled download pattern once the helper exists, because they share the same desktop-shell failure mode
